reasoning: As of the knowledge cutoff (October 26, 2025), Paul Biedermann's 200m freestyle world record (1:42.00, set in 2009) remains standing according to the Wikipedia 'List of world records in swimming'. The record survived the major international long course event of the year, the 2025 World Aquatics Championships in Singapore (July/August 2025). The period from late October to January typically focuses on short course (25m) swimming, including the Swimming World Cup and year-end national championships. Long course world records are rarely broken during this off-season window, especially a storied mark like the 200m free which has not been bettered in textile suits since 2009. Given the absence of major long course championships before January 2026 and the difficulty of the record itself, the probability of it being broken remains extremely low in the near term. My confidence decreases slightly for the January date to account for potential early-season long course meets in the Southern Hemisphere (e.g., Australian regional championships), though the risk remains low.
